question: Will Elon Musk post 640-659 tweets in April 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of April 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket centers on Elon Musk posting 1320-1439 tweets in April 2026, with the 1360-1399 range leading at 7.8% implied probability and 1400-1439 close behind at 7.0%, reflecting an extrapolated daily average of 44-48 posts. This stems from Musk's surging tweet volume—often exceeding 1,400 monthly amid political clashes, Tesla milestones, and xAI hype—mirroring peaks in late 2024 like November's near-2,000 total. Competitive dynamics hinge on whether traders anticipate sustained hyper-engagement from his pop culture dominance and X ownership or a modest dip from fatigue or focus shifts, with lower bins fading amid historical upward trends in his unfiltered online presence.

This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of April 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

question: Set Handicap: Masarova (-1.5) vs Osuigwe (+1.5)
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Rebeka Masarova and Whitney Osuigwe in the ATX Open, scheduled for February 25 2026.
This market will resolve to "Masarova" if Rebeka Masarova wins by 2 or more sets than Whitney Osuigwe, based on the final completed score. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Osuigwe."
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA results.
groupItemTitle: ATX Open: Rebeka Masarova vs Whitney Osuigwe Set Handicap +/-1.5

events.title: ATX Open: Rebeka Masarova vs Whitney Osuigwe
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Rebeka Masarova and Whitney Osuigwe in the ATX Open, scheduled for February 25 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Masarova' if Rebeka Masarova advances against Whitney Osuigwe.

This market will resolve to 'Osuigwe' if Whitney Osuigwe advances against Rebeka Masarova.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rebeka Masarova enters as the clear favorite in her ATX Open first-round clash against Whitney Osuigwe, with trader consensus implying around 75% win probability for the Spaniard, driven by her superior ranking (No. 109 vs. Osuigwe's No. 565) and consistent hardcourt results this season, including a recent quarterfinal run in Puerto Vallarta. Osuigwe, an American wildcard riding qualifier momentum, brings home-crowd energy but faces a steep form gap after injury setbacks, with no head-to-head history. Key watch: Masarova's baseline power versus Osuigwe's aggressive returns; no reported injuries shift the edge, though early-round upsets on fast Austin hardcourts add upset risk amid thin schedules ahead.

question: Will Leicester Tigers win?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for March 22 2026
If Leicester Tigers wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "No".
This market refers only to the outcome when the ball becomes dead after 80 minutes of regular play.

events.title: Premiership Rugby: Leicester Tigers vs Bristol Bears
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Premiership Rugby match between Leicester Tigers and Bristol Bears, scheduled for March 22 2026.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' near-even split on Leicester Tigers (49.5%) and Bristol Bears (49.5%) underscores a fiercely balanced Premiership Rugby showdown, driven by both teams' red-hot form with four wins in five Gallagher Premiership starts this season. Leicester's home edge at Mattioli Woods Welford Road, bolstered by Handré Pollard's fly-half mastery and a robust scrum, counters Bristol's explosive backline firepower from Max Malins and Piers O'Connon. Minimal injury disruptions—key forwards like Hanro Liebenberg and Jake Heeney available—keep lineups potent, while recent head-to-heads (split 2-2 in last four) and high-scoring trends amplify draw risk at 4.5%. Momentum favors neither decisively, mirroring the wisdom of crowds in this playoff-pivotal clash.

eventMetadata.context_description: Union Bordeaux Bègles holds a narrow trader edge over Toulouse in this pivotal Top 14 matchup, fueled by UBB's formidable home form at Stade Chaban-Delmas—where they've won eight straight—and recent momentum from gritty victories against top sides like Racing 92. Toulouse counters with superior squad depth, a dominant head-to-head record (winning four of the last five), and explosive backline play led by fit stars like Antoine Dupont. No major injuries disrupt either lineup per official reports, leaving set-piece battles and breakdown intensity as key swing factors in a contest both teams desperately need for playoff positioning, keeping odds tightly bunched.

question: Will Toulouse win?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for March 22 2026
If Toulouse wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "No".
This market refers only to the outcome when the ball becomes dead after 80 minutes of regular play.

question: Spread: CA River Plate (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for March 8 at 8:15 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "CA River Plate" if CA River Plate win the game by 2 or more goals.

events.title: CA River Plate vs. CA Tucumán - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for March 8 at 8:15 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: River Plate's formidable home form at Estadio Monumental, where they've won 8 of their last 10 league matches, anchors trader sentiment heavily in their favor against struggling Atlético Tucumán, implying strong probabilities for a River victory across win, spread, and total goals markets. Recent developments bolster this: River sit atop the Primera División table after a 2-0 midweek win over Godoy Cruz, with key attackers Miguel Borja and Pablo Solari fit per official reports, while Tucumán languish in the relegation zone, winless in six away games and missing midfielder Marcelo Estigarribia due to suspension. Upcoming factors include River's rest edge post-international break and Tucumán's grueling travel, though River's occasional defensive lapses leave room for upset value.

events.title: CA Central Córdoba vs. CA Boca Juniors - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for March 8 at 6:15 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Boca Juniors hold strong trader consensus as favorites against Central Córdoba, reflecting their superior league standing and head-to-head edge, with five wins in the last seven meetings. Recent form bolsters this: Boca sit third in the Primera División, unbeaten in four straight away games, while Central Córdoba languish near relegation, winless in six home matches amid defensive frailties conceding 12 goals. No major injuries reported for Boca's key attackers like Merentiel, per official updates, but Central Córdoba's midfielder Atencio is doubtful with a hamstring strain. Upcoming weather in Santiago del Estero could favor Boca's technical style, though crowd energy adds upset risk—markets price Boca win at 65% implied probability.

question: Will Russia enter Stinky by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Stinky, Donetsk Oblast, (48.578829902717374° N, 37.73960567273168° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

question: Will $2.50 Coin become law this year?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if legislation that would require the Treasury to mint and issue $2.50 coins to mark America’s 250th anniversary is passed by both chambers of the U.S. Congress and signed into law by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Qualifying legislation includes the H.R. 5616 (119th) — “$2.50 for America’s 250th Act”. 

Qualifying legislation may include joint resolutions and must pass both the House and the Senate, and must be signed by the President, become law without signature while Congress remains in session, or become law through veto override. Presidential pocket vetoes that expire resolve to "No".

The primary resolution sources for this market will be Congress.gov’s legislation tracker (https://www.congress.gov/bill/119th-congress/house-bill/22), the Library of Congress (congress.gov), and other official information from the government of the United States; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Will Film/TV production expensing become law this year?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if legislation that extends Section 181 expensing for film and television productions is passed by both chambers of the U.S. Congress and signed into law by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Qualifying legislation includes H.R. 4787 (119th).

Qualifying legislation may include joint resolutions and must pass both the House and the Senate, and must be signed by the President, become law without signature while Congress remains in session, or become law through veto override. Presidential pocket vetoes that expire resolve to "No".

The primary resolution sources for this market will be Congress.gov’s legislation tracker (https://www.congress.gov/bill/119th-congress/house-bill/22), the Library of Congress (congress.gov), and other official information from the government of the United States; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Will Justin McNeal be the Republican nominee for Senate in South Dakota?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from South Dakota.

If no 2026 South Dakota Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the South Dakota Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: South Dakota Republican Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from South Dakota.

If no 2026 South Dakota Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the South Dakota Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Senator Mike Rounds dominates trader consensus at 88.5% implied probability for the South Dakota Republican Senate primary, bolstered by his strong reelection announcement in April 2024, robust fundraising exceeding $1 million early, and historical base rates favoring incumbents in deep-red states like South Dakota, where no sitting senator has lost a primary since 2004. Governor Kristi Noem trails at 6.9% amid speculation she may prioritize her term or national ambitions over a Senate bid, complicated by recent controversies from her memoir that have softened her polling edge. Challenger Justin McNeal holds 3.5% with minimal visibility and resources, as no major polls or endorsements have emerged to shift dynamics ahead of the 2026 primary.

question: Will Jeff Hurd be the Republican nominee for CO-03?
description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Republican Party to contest the CO-03 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Republican primary will take place on June 30, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican sources, including https://www.rnc.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: CO-03 Republican Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Republican Party to contest the CO-03 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Republican primary will take place on June 30, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican sources, including https://www.rnc.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jeff Hurd's commanding 97.9% implied probability in the Colorado 3rd District Republican primary stems from his dominant performance in the June 25 election results, where he secured approximately 75% of the vote against Hope Scheppelman amid strong county-level leads in key areas like Pueblo and Mesa. Hurd's advantages include superior fundraising exceeding $1.5 million, endorsements from the National Republican Congressional Committee, local GOP leaders, and a late Trump nod, bolstering his establishment-conservative appeal in the district's rural and Western Slope base. Scheppelman, emphasizing harder-line immigration stances, trailed significantly in pre-election polls. Realistic challenges would require substantiated irregularities prompting a recount, though Hurd's wide margin makes certification all but certain.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trump's August 9 endorsement of John Shulli via Truth Social has driven his lead to 54.5% trader consensus in the Delaware Republican U.S. Senate primary market, up from prior parity with Michael Katz at 44%. Recent polling, including a mid-August University of Delaware survey showing Shulli at 37% to Katz's 28%, bolsters this edge ahead of the September 10 contest. Shulli's advantages include superior fundraising—$245,000 raised versus Katz's $178,000 through July—and backing from the National Republican Senatorial Committee, appealing to GOP base voters in the blue state. Traders discount minor candidates, focusing on these frontrunners' momentum from national support and local turnout potential.

question: Jets vs. Rangers
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for March 22 at 12:00PM ET:
If the Jets win, the market will resolve to "Jets".
If the Rangers win, the market will resolve to "Rangers".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

Upcoming factors: Jets' road woes (2-3 away) versus Rangers' 5-1 home mark, plus power-play disparity favoring New York at 28.6% efficiency. Upsets remain possible given the Jets' rest advantage.

In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for March 22 at 3:00PM ET:
If the Predators win, the market will resolve to "Predators".
If the Blackhawks win, the market will resolve to "Blackhawks".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Predators hold a strong edge in trader consensus for their matchup against the Blackhawks, fueled by Nashville's superior 5-2-1 start compared to Chicago's dismal 2-6 record amid a rebuilding phase. Recent developments include Filip Forsberg's return from a minor injury for the Predators, bolstering their top line, while Chicago remains without captain Nick Foligno (suspended) and faces goaltending woes with Arvid Soderblom struggling at .878 save percentage. Nashville's home-ice advantage at Bridgestone Arena and rest edge after a lighter schedule further tilt implied probabilities, though Blackhawks' young stars like Connor Bedard could spark an upset in this Central Division clash.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus leans toward the New York Islanders in their home matchup against the Columbus Blue Jackets, driven by New York's 4-2-1 start and stingy penalty kill ranking top-five league-wide, contrasting Columbus's 2-4-1 record marred by defensive lapses allowing 4.1 goals per game. Critical recent development: Blue Jackets' top scorer Kirill Marchenko is questionable with an upper-body injury per official report, potentially weakening their power play that's converted just 12%. Islanders gain rest advantage post-back-to-back, with Ilya Sorokin 3-1-0 at UBS Arena. Head-to-head, Isles won last three, but Columbus's road urgency amid playoff longshot status adds upset potential. Schedule ahead features Isles' softer slate.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vegas Golden Knights' goaltending edge with Adin Hill's .930 save percentage in recent playoff games anchors trader consensus favoring them over the Dallas Stars, who trail 3-2 in this first-round series after a 2-1 Game 5 loss. Stone's upper-body injury clouds Vegas' top line production, but their 7-2-1 record in last 10 home games bolsters implied probabilities around 60% for a Knights win in Game 6. Dallas counters with Oettinger's 2.10 GAA and Robertson's five goals, yet fatigue from a grueling schedule tempers optimism amid Vegas' rest advantage and superior power play at 28%. Head-to-head splits show Vegas 4-1 SU in last five meetings.

eventMetadata.context_description: Buffalo Sabres hold the edge in trader consensus for their home clash with the Anaheim Ducks, fueled by a three-game win streak including a 4-2 victory over New Jersey, contrasting Anaheim's 1-3-1 skid marked by defensive lapses allowing 3.8 goals per game lately. Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen's .930 save percentage in recent starts bolsters Buffalo's netminding advantage over Anaheim's tandem of Dostal and Wolf, who face 34 shots nightly. No major injuries reported for either side per official updates, but Ducks' road woes (0-3-1 away) and Sabres' home dominance (3-1-0 at KeyBank Center) amplify the implied probabilities, though Anaheim's counterattack remains a wildcard upset threat.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Rangers hold a clear edge as home favorites against the Ottawa Senators, with trader consensus implying around 65% win probability for NYR amid the Senators' ongoing rebuild and poor road record (3-5-1 away). Recent developments include Rangers goaltender Igor Shesterkin returning from minor maintenance for his 10th straight start, bolstering their top-ranked penalty kill, while Ottawa's Tim Stützle remains sidelined with an upper-body injury per official reports, weakening their top line. Senators snapped a three-game skid with a 5-2 win over Anaheim but face fatigue from back-to-back games; Rangers boast rest advantage and 7-2-1 home form. Head-to-head, NYR has won four of the last five, underscoring momentum driving current odds.

question: Will Allbirds (BIRD) beat quarterly earnings?
description:
As of market creation, Allbirds is estimated to release earnings on March 10, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Allbirds's GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $-2.28 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Allbirds reports GAAP EPS greater than $-2.28 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Allbirds releases earnings without GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve to “No”.

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for GAAP EPS. 
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless this is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

question: Larry Wheels divorce by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Larry Wheels and/or his spouse announce their intention to divorce by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement by the specified date of their intention to divorce will be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the divorce later actually occurs, or whether the actual divorce occurs outside of the timeframe of this market.

The resolution source will be statements from Larry Wheels and/or his spouse, and their representatives, however a definitive consensus of credible media reporting may be considered.

question: Will fewer than 950 tornadoes occur in the United States in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of tornadoes recorded in the United States in 2026, based on the monthly counts published on the National Centers for Environmental Information U.S. Tornadoes Time Series page (see: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/monitoring/tornadoes/time-series).

Only tornadoes appearing in the final NCEI dataset for all months of 2026 will count.

As of market creation, the December report is not yet scheduled, however the release schedule can be found here: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/monitoring/dyk/monthly-releases. The market will resolve based on the first relevant tornado count published on the NCEI tornado time-series page after this scheduled release time.

If the value published after this scheduled release time is labeled preliminary, it will still determine resolution, and the market will resolve independently of any subsequent revisions, corrections, or retroactive adjustments. 

The market will not resolve based on any preliminary values published before the scheduled release time.

If no data is published by the scheduled release time, or if the NCEI website is temporarily unavailable, this market will remain open until that data is made available. If the relevant data is not made available by the date of the next scheduled publication ET, this market will resolve based on available data for the most recent prior month. If the NCEI website becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve using another credible source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment favors 1250+ US tornadoes in 2026 at 39% implied probability, driven by NOAA's Storm Prediction Center data showing recent years far exceeding the long-term average of about 1,200: 2023 confirmed 1,382 and 2024 preliminary counts surpassing 1,700 amid widespread outbreaks. Enhanced convective available potential energy (CAPE) from atmospheric warming boosts thunderstorm intensity, while transitional ENSO phases—like the shift from 2024's neutral conditions—historically correlate with elevated Plains activity. However, declining wind shear could temper totals, and annual forecasts remain uncertain due to model divergences; traders eye spring 2025 outlooks for refinement.

question: Rinderknech vs. Draper: Set 1 Games O/U 8.5
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Arthur Rinderknech and Jack Draper in the Dubai Tennis Championships, scheduled for February 25 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 9. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP results.
groupItemTitle: Dubai Tennis Championships: Arthur Rinderknech vs Jack Draper Set 1 O/U 8.5

events.title: Dubai Tennis Championships: Arthur Rinderknech vs Jack Draper
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Arthur Rinderknech and Jack Draper in the Dubai Tennis Championships, scheduled for February 25 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Rinderknech' if Arthur Rinderknech advances against Jack Draper.

This market will resolve to 'Draper' if Jack Draper advances against Arthur Rinderknech.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jack Draper's superior recent form on hard courts drives trader consensus favoring him over Arthur Rinderknech in their Dubai Tennis Championships Round of 16 clash, with implied probabilities reflecting his world No. 56 ranking and efficient 6-1, 6-2 win over Alexandre Muller. Rinderknech, ranked No. 75, stunned top seed Andrey Rublev 7-5, 3-6, 7-6(6) in a grueling three-setter, boosting his confidence but exposing potential fatigue after three qualifying matches. No injuries reported for either left-handed player in their first head-to-head; Draper's stronger serve and youth (22 vs. 28) could exploit Rinderknech's inconsistency, though the Frenchman's baseline resilience adds upset risk on fast Dubai hard courts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment favors 1250+ US tornadoes in 2026 at 39% implied probability, driven by NOAA's Storm Prediction Center data showing recent years far exceeding the long-term average of about 1,200: 2023 confirmed 1,382 and 2024 preliminary counts surpassing 1,700 amid widespread outbreaks. question: Will 1200 to 1249 tornadoes occur in the United States in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of tornadoes recorded in the United States in 2026, based on the monthly counts published on the National Centers for Environmental Information U.S. Tornadoes Time Series page (see: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/monitoring/tornadoes/time-series).

Only tornadoes appearing in the final NCEI dataset for all months of 2026 will count.

As of market creation, the December report is not yet scheduled, however the release schedule can be found here: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/monitoring/dyk/monthly-releases. The market will resolve based on the first relevant tornado count published on the NCEI tornado time-series page after this scheduled release time.

If the value published after this scheduled release time is labeled preliminary, it will still determine resolution, and the market will resolve independently of any subsequent revisions, corrections, or retroactive adjustments. 

The market will not resolve based on any preliminary values published before the scheduled release time.

If the relevant data is not made available by the date of the next scheduled publication ET, this market will resolve based on available data for the most recent prior month. If the NCEI website becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve using another credible source.

question: Will Trump declare war on Iran by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President Donald Trump or an official representative of his administration (such as the White House Press Secretary, National Security Advisor, or Secretary of Defense) issues a formal public statement declaring that the United States is at war with Iran by the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The statement must clearly and explicitly use language equivalent to a declaration of war—for example, stating that "the United States is now at war with Iran" or "we are declaring war on Iran."

General statements about military action, retaliation, force, or operations will not qualify unless they include an unambiguous declaration of war.

Executive orders or official memoranda may qualify only if they contain clear language declaring war.

The primary resolution source will be official government communications, including White House press releases, presidential speeches, or public statements by senior administration officials; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects low implied probability for President-elect Trump declaring war on Iran by the specified date, absent any acute military provocation amid simmering US-Iran tensions. Recent Israeli airstrikes on Iranian facilities in October 2024 elicited measured responses without US escalation under Biden, and Trump has historically favored sanctions and targeted actions—like the 2020 Soleimani strike—over full-scale conflict. His "America First" stance prioritizes avoiding new Middle East wars, with constitutional war powers residing in Congress. Key upcoming events include Trump's January 20, 2025, inauguration and national security appointments, which could signal policy shifts but have yet to alter market sentiment significantly.

events.title: Estudiantes de La Plata vs. CA Central Córdoba
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for Monday, March 23, 2026 between Estudiantes de La Plata and CA Central Córdoba.

eventMetadata.context_description: Estudiantes de La Plata's robust home record and superior recent form underpin their 57% implied probability as trader consensus leaders against CA Central Córdoba, who sit near the relegation zone with just one win in their last 10 league outings. The hosts have won three of their past five home matches, bolstered by key midfielder Santiago Ascacíbar's return from suspension, while Central Córdoba grapples with forward Nicolas Laméndola's confirmed hamstring injury and a porous defense conceding 2.1 goals per away game. A draw at 26.5% gains traction from Central Córdoba's low-scoring stalemates in five of eight road trips, though Estudiantes' attacking momentum—three goals in each of their last two wins—tempers upset chances at 14.5%. Schedule rest favors the hosts ahead of this mid-table clash.

question: Will Aaron Kahng win The Bachelorette Season 22?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of The Bachelorette Season 22. 

The winner is defined as the contestant who receives the final rose from the Bachelorette. Any changes in relationship status after the final rose ceremony including the "After the Final Rose" segment will not be considered. 

If no rose is given, or the finale ends without a final rose ceremony, this market will resolve to "Other". 

If the final episode of the bachelorette is not publicly available by November 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other". 

The official resolution source will be the finale episode of The Bachelorette Season 22.

This market will resolve to the player who records the most assists through all main tournament rounds of the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League competition.
		
In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by UEFA Europa Conference League rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025-26 Europa Conference League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: UEFA Europa Conference League: Most Assists
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the UEFA Europa Conference League most assists market clusters tightly around 44% for leaders Carlo Holse, Orbelín Pineda, Martial Godo, Lee Jae-Sung, Lazaros Rota, and Jean-Philippe Mateta, reflecting their shared tally of three assists each after four to five league phase matches. This parity stems from balanced chance creation across teams like AEK Athens, Celtic, PAOK, and Standard Liège, where wingers and attacking midfielders have thrived amid high-possession styles and set-piece prowess. Recent outings, including Pineda's key pass in AEK's midweek victory and Godo's crossing threat for Celtic, sustained the deadlock without a standout surge. With one league phase game remaining plus potential playoffs, traders weigh similar fixture difficulties and rotation risks, keeping the race fluid and unpredictable.
